SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(microseconds, 'UTC') AS utc_time FROM test_timestamps;
SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(microseconds, 'America/New_York') AS ny_time FROM test_timestamps;
SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(microseconds) AS default_time FROM test_timestamps;
SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(microseconds + 72000000000, 'Asia/Tokyo') AS tokyo_time FROM test_timestamps;
WITH (SELECT MAX(microseconds) FROM test_timestamps) AS max_microseconds SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(max_microseconds) AS max_time;
WITH CAST(1234567891011 AS Int64) AS micro_value SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(micro_value) AS converted_time;
SELECT FROM test_timestamps WHERE fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(microseconds, 'Europe/London') > '2023-01-01 00:00:00' AND microseconds < 0;
SELECT fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(microseconds) AS precision_test FROM (SELECT microseconds FROM test_timestamps) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE joined_timestamps (id Int64, timestamp Int64);
SELECT t.id, fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(t.timestamp, 'UTC') AS converted_time FROM joined_timestamps AS t JOIN test_timestamps AS ts ON ts.microseconds > 0;
